0.original: A few months ago, a former student of mine, in business for just four years, opened her fourth hardware store.
0.b1: A few months ago, a former student of mine opened her fourth hardware store after only four years in business.
0.cn: 几个月前，我的一位前学生开了她的第四家五金店，而她创业才不过四年。
0.ai_notes:
'a few months ago': a short time before now.
'in business': working as a professional or running a company.

1.original: This was quite a feat, considering the young lady's small initial capital investment of only $3,500,
1.b1: This was a great achievement, considering she started with only $3,500.
1.cn: 考虑到她最初的创业资金只有3500美元，这真是一项了不起的成就。
1.ai_notes:
'feat': a difficult achievement that requires skill or strength.
'initial capital investment': the money used to start a business.

2.original: strong competition from other stores, and the relatively short time she had been in business.
2.b1: She also faced strong competition and had very little experience.
2.cn: 而且她还面临着激烈的竞争，创业时间也相对较短。
2.ai_notes: 'strong competition': when many other businesses are selling similar things and fighting for the same customers.

3.original: I visited her new store shortly after it opened, to congratulate her on the fine progress she had made.
3.b1: I visited her new store to congratulate her on her success.
3.cn: 我去了她的新店，祝贺她取得了这么好的成绩。
3.ai_notes:
'congratulate': to tell someone you are happy for their success.
'fine progress': good improvement or success.

4.original: In an indirect way, I asked her how she was able to make a success of three stores and open a fourth one,
4.b1: I asked her how she managed to run three successful stores and open a fourth one.
4.cn: 我委婉地问她，是如何成功经营三家店并开设第四家店的。
4.ai_notes:
'in an indirect way': not asking directly; being polite or subtle.
'make a success of': to make something successful.

5.original: when most merchants had to struggle to make a success of just one store.
5.b1: Most business owners find it hard to succeed with even one store.
5.cn: 因为大多数店主经营一家店就已经很吃力了。
5.ai_notes:
'merchants': people who buy and sell goods; shop owners.
'struggle': to try very hard to do something difficult.

6.original: Naturally, she answered, I worked hard, but just getting up early and working late isn't responsible for the four stores.
6.b1: She said, "I work hard, but working long hours is not the only reason for my success."
6.cn: 她回答说：“我确实很努力，但仅仅靠起早贪黑工作是无法拥有这四家店的。”
6.ai_notes:
'naturally': used to show that something is expected or logical.
'responsible for': being the cause of something.

7.original: Most people in my business work hard.
7.b1: Most people in my industry work hard.
7.cn: 我这一行的大多数人都很努力。
7.ai_notes: 'in my business': in my industry or field of work.

8.original: The main thing I attribute my success to is my self-styled weekly improvement program.
8.b1: My success comes from my own weekly improvement program.
8.cn: 我成功的主要原因归功于我自创的每周改进计划。
8.ai_notes:
'attribute my success to': to say that success is caused by a specific thing.
'self-styled': created or named by oneself.

9.ai_notes: 'weekly improvement program': a routine of checking and changing things every week to get better results.

10.ai_notes: 'impressive': something that makes you feel admiration because it is good or large.

11.ai_notes: 'How does it work?': a common way to ask for an explanation of a process or system.

12.ai_notes: 'I asked': a simple tag used to show who is speaking in a story.

13.original: Well, it really isn't anything elaborate, she continued.
13.b1: She said it was not very complicated.
13.cn: 她接着说，其实这并不复杂。
13.ai_notes: 'elaborate': complicated or having many detailed parts.

14.original: It's just a plan to help me do a better job as each week rolls around.
14.b1: It is just a plan to help me work better every week.
14.cn: 这只是一个帮助我每周都能做得更好的计划。
14.ai_notes: 'as each week rolls around': as each new week begins.

15.original: To keep my forward thinking on the track, I've divided my job into four elements,
15.b1: To stay focused, I divided my work into four parts:
15.cn: 为了保持进步，我把工作分成了四个部分：
15.ai_notes:
'forward thinking': planning for the future.
'on the track': moving in the right direction or staying focused.

16.ai_notes:
'merchandise': goods or products for sale.
'promotion': activities to advertise or encourage the sale of products.

17.original: All during the week I make notes and jot down ideas as to how I can improve my business.
17.b1: All week, I write down ideas on how to improve my business.
17.cn: 整个星期，我都会记下关于如何改进业务的想法。

18.original: Then, every Monday evening, I set aside four hours to review the ideas I've jotted down and figure out how to put the solid ones to use in the business.
18.b1: Every Monday night, I spend four hours reviewing these ideas and planning how to use them.
18.cn: 然后，每周一晚上，我都会花四个小时来回顾这些想法，并找出如何将好的点子应用到生意中。
18.ai_notes:
'set aside': to reserve time or money for a specific purpose.
'put to use': to start using something.

19.original: In this four-hour period, I force myself to take a hard look at my operation.
19.b1: During these four hours, I carefully examine how my business is running.
19.cn: 在这四个小时里，我强迫自己认真审视我的经营状况。
19.ai_notes:
'take a hard look at': to examine something very critically and honestly.
'operation': the way a business is run.

20.original: I don't simply wish more customers would shop in my store.
20.b1: I don't just hope for more customers.
20.cn: 我不仅仅是希望有更多顾客来购物。
20.ai_notes: 'wish': to hope for something that might not happen.

21.original: Instead, I ask myself, what can I do to attract more customers?
21.b1: Instead, I ask myself what I can do to attract them.
21.cn: 相反，我会问自己，我能做些什么来吸引更多顾客？
21.ai_notes: 'attract': to make people want to come to a place.

22.original: How can I develop regular, loyal customers?
22.b1: How can I make them loyal customers?
22.cn: 我该如何培养忠实的常客？
22.ai_notes:
'develop': to grow or create something over time.
'loyal customers': people who keep coming back to the same store.

23.original: She went on describing numerous little innovations that made her first three stores so successful.
23.b1: She described many small changes that made her first three stores successful.
23.cn: 她接着描述了许多让前三家店取得成功的小创新。
23.ai_notes:
'went on': continued speaking.
'innovations': new ideas, methods, or products.

24.original: Things like the way she arranged the merchandise within her stores, her suggestion selling technique that sold two out of three customers merchandise they had not planned to buy when they entered her stores.
24.b1: For example, she changed how she displayed products and used techniques to sell more items to customers.
24.cn: 比如她陈列商品的方式，以及她的建议销售技巧，让三分之二的顾客买到了他们原本没打算买的东西。
24.ai_notes:
'suggestion selling': a technique where a salesperson suggests extra items to a customer.
'planned to buy': intended to purchase.

25.original: The credit plan she devised when many of her customers were out of work because of a strike.
25.b1: She also created a credit plan when customers were out of work due to a strike.
25.cn: 还有她在罢工导致许多顾客失业时设计的信贷计划。
25.ai_notes:
'credit plan': a system allowing customers to pay for goods later.
'strike': when workers stop working to protest for better pay or conditions.

26.original: The contest she developed that boosted sales during a slack season.
26.b1: She even held contests to increase sales during slow seasons.
26.cn: 以及她在淡季时为了促进销售而举办的竞赛。
26.ai_notes:
'boosted': increased or improved.
'slack season': a time when business is slow.

27.original: I ask myself, what can I do to improve my merchandise offerings?
27.b1: I ask myself, how can I improve the products I offer?
27.cn: 我问自己，我能做些什么来改善我的商品供应？
27.ai_notes: 'merchandise offerings': the products a store has available for customers to buy.

28.cn: 然后我就会得到新的点子。
28.ai_notes: 'get ideas': to think of new plans or solutions.

29.original: Let me cite just one case.
29.b1: Let me give you an example.

30.original: Four weeks ago, it occurred to me that I should do something to get more youngsters into the store.
30.b1: Four weeks ago, I thought about how to get more children into my store.
30.cn: 四周前，我想到应该做点什么来吸引更多年轻人进店。
30.ai_notes: 'it occurred to me': I suddenly had an idea.

31.original: I reasoned if I had something here to draw the kids to the store,
31.b1: I thought if I had something to attract the kids,
31.cn: 我想如果我店里有吸引孩子的东西，

32.original: I'd also draw more of the parents.
32.b1: I would also attract their parents.
32.cn: 我也能吸引他们的父母。
32.ai_notes: 'draw': to attract or pull people toward something.

33.original: I kept thinking about it, and then this idea came.
33.b1: I kept thinking, and then I had an idea.
33.cn: 我一直在思考，然后就想到了这个主意。
33.ai_notes: 'this idea came': I thought of this idea.

34.original: Put in a line of small carted toys for children in the four to eight age bracket.
34.b1: I decided to sell small toys for children aged four to eight.
34.cn: 引进一批适合四到八岁儿童的小玩具。
34.ai_notes:
'line': a range of products.
'age bracket': a specific range of ages.

36.original: The toys take little space, and I make a nice profit on them.
36.b1: The toys take up little space and make a good profit.
36.cn: 玩具占地很少，而且利润不错。
36.ai_notes: 'make a nice profit': to earn a good amount of money after costs.

37.original: But most important, the toys have increased store traffic.
37.b1: Most importantly, they bring more people into the store.
37.cn: 但最重要的是，玩具增加了店里的客流量。
37.ai_notes: 'store traffic': the number of people visiting a store.

38.original: Believe me, she went on, my weekly improvement plan works.
38.b1: She said her weekly plan really works.
38.cn: 她继续说，相信我，我的每周改进计划真的有效。
38.ai_notes: 'Believe me': a phrase used to emphasize that what you are saying is true.

39.original: Just by conscientiously asking myself, how can I do a better job, I find the answers.
39.b1: By asking myself how to do a better job, I find the answers.
39.cn: 只要认真地问自己如何才能做得更好，我就能找到答案。
39.ai_notes: 'conscientiously': in a careful and thorough way.

40.original: It's a rare Monday night that I don't come up with some plan or technique that makes that profit and loss statement look better.
40.b1: Almost every Monday, I find a way to make my business more profitable.
40.cn: 几乎每个周一晚上，我都能想出一些计划或技巧，让财务报表看起来更好看。
40.ai_notes:
'come up with': to think of an idea or plan.
'profit and loss statement': a financial document showing if a business is making money.

41.original: And I've learned something else, too, about successful merchandising, something that I think every person going into business for himself should know.
41.b1: I also learned something important about business that everyone should know.
41.cn: 我还学到了关于成功经营的另一件事，我认为每个创业者都应该知道。
41.ai_notes:
'merchandising': the activity of promoting and selling products.
'going into business for himself': starting his own company.

42.ai_notes: 'What's that?': a short way to ask for more information about a previous statement.

43.original: Just this, it isn't so much what you know when you start that matters,
43.b1: She said, "It is not about what you know when you start."
43.cn: 她说：“就是这个，创业时你知道什么并不重要，”
43.ai_notes: 'it isn't so much... that matters': it is not the most important thing.

44.original: it's what you learn and put to use after you open your doors that counts most.
44.b1: It is about what you learn and use after you open your business.
44.cn: 重要的是开业后你学到了什么并将其付诸实践。
44.ai_notes:
'open your doors': to start a business.
'counts': is important.
